ogTitle: Alexander Heights vs Alexandra Bridge: Climate Safety

heading: Alexander Heights vs Alexandra Bridge — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Alexander Heights, Western Australia and Alexandra Bridge, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Alexandra Bridge has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Alexander Heights (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Alexander Heights or Alexandra Bridge safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Alexander Heights has a flood risk score of 57/100, while Alexandra Bridge scores 20/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Alexander Heights scores 21/100 for bushfire risk. Alexandra Bridge scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Alexander Heights or Alexandra Bridge?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Alexandra Bridge has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
